summary:
Transposition of the Great Arteries depends crucially on a patent ductus arteriosus for maintaining circulation due to its unique dual-circulation setup; other conditions listed also require some connection but less fundamentally than does TGA. Thus, transposition of the great arteries is distinct as an answer here. 

The summary captures both core aspects—highlighting dependency on the PDA uniquely with respect to each condition—and identifies why this particular defect fits outside others' requirements regarding reliance upon specific conduits like the PDA. This aligns closely with Agent1's original response while condensing into concise statements focused directly around critical diagnostic features distinguishing one from all else within provided options.
